stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM release.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformers, text-generation, and the Llama family.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It points to the model's relevance within the text-generation pipeline, indicating its capability for language tasks.
The associated tags confirm its foundation in the transformers architecture and its connection to the Llama family of models.

stories.title: “Will I be OK?” Teen died after ChatGPT pushed deadly mix of drugs, lawsuit says
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A teenager died after using ChatGPT to guide him in experimenting with a dangerous drug mixture.
The incident highlights critical safety and ethical failures in large language models (LLMs) regarding harmful content generation.
The case has resulted in a lawsuit, indicating potential legal and regulatory scrutiny of AI safety.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML frameworks like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling an optimization push for local AI on Apple devices.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all processing runs entirely on the user's local device, emphasizing privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, running locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership also involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools), emphasizing open safety tooling.

stories.title: Alexa is moving into Amazon.com
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Amazon is integrating its LLM-powered AI assistant, Alexa Plus, directly into the Amazon.com shopping experience.
The new feature, 'Alexa for Shopping,' allows users to interact with the platform using conversational queries.
This represents a significant shift in how Amazon is leveraging AI to enhance its core e-commerce functionality.

stories.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
stories.author: Hengshuang Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces AlphaGRPO, a new framework applying Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to AR-Diffusion Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs).
Enhances multimodal generation capabilities by enabling advanced reasoning tasks without requiring an extra cold-start stage.
Unlocks self-reflective capabilities, allowing models to infer implicit user intents and autonomously refine their own outputs.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces Attractor Models as a novel approach to overcome limitations of recurrent architectures in AI.
Attractor Models refine latent representations by solving for a fixed point, offering an alternative to purely feed-forward computation.
The method aims to improve language modeling and reasoning while addressing training instability and deployment costs associated with traditional recurrent models.

stories.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
stories.author: Hengshuang Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces AlphaGRPO, a novel framework applying Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs).
Enhances multimodal generation by enabling advanced reasoning tasks like inferring implicit user intents in text-to-image generation.
Features Self-Reflective Refinement, allowing the model to autonomously diagnose and correct its own outputs without extra training stages.

stories.title: Elastic Attention Cores for Scalable Vision Transformers
stories.author: Andrew F. Luo
bullet_points: The paper addresses the quadratic computational cost of standard Vision Transformers (ViTs) when processing high-resolution images.
It challenges the core assumption that all pairwise token interactions (all-to-all self-attention) are necessary for effective visual representation learning.
The proposed 'Elastic Attention Cores' aim to enable scalable ViT architectures for high-resolution domains by reducing computational complexity.

stories.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance
stories.author: Assaf Toledo
bullet_points: Proposes a novel paradigm where a generative LLM guides the refinement of text embeddings.
The method allows embedding models to adapt in real-time to specific, challenging zero-shot search and classification tasks.
Tests the approach extensively using state-of-the-art text embedding models across diverse benchmarks.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a novel architecture to improve language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of recurrent architectures (instability, high cost, fixed depth) while offering an alternative to purely feed-forward models.
The model refines initial output embeddings by solving for a fixed point using an attractor module.

stories.title: Multi-Stream LLMs: Unblocking Language Models with Parallel Streams of Thoughts, Inputs and Outputs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Multi-Stream LLMs' to overcome the computational bottleneck inherent in current sequential LLM architectures.
Current advanced AI agents rely on a single stream of computation (e.g., chain-of-thought) for message exchange with users, tools, and themselves.
The research aims to enhance LLM capabilities beyond simple instruction tuning by introducing parallel streams for thoughts, inputs, and outputs.

stories.title: TextSeal: A Localized LLM Watermark for Provenance & Distillation Protection
stories.author: Pierre Fernandez
bullet_points: TextSeal is a new, state-of-the-art LLM watermark designed for provenance tracking and protecting against model distillation.
It utilizes dual-key generation and entropy-weighted scoring, achieving superior detection strength compared to baselines like SynthID-text.
The method is optimized for serving efficiency, adding no inference overhead while supporting advanced decoding techniques.

stories.title: The Algorithmic Caricature: Auditing LLM-Generated Political Discourse Across Crisis Events
stories.author: Talal Rahwan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the growing risk of synthetic political discourse generated by LLMs during real-world crises.
It critiques current AI-text detection methods (like perplexity) as insufficient against increasingly sophisticated generative models.
The research proposes a novel approach using Computational Social Science to determine if synthetic discourse mimics observed online population behavior.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It enhances the flexibility of open-source local LLM deployments by adding compatibility with proprietary APIs.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code in a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Canon’s latest EOS R6 camera goes V for video
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: Canon has released the EOS R6 V mirrorless camera, specifically designed with video capture as its primary focus.
The camera features a 32.5-megapixel sensor and supports 7K video recording.
It is based on the EOS R6 Mark III but notably omits features such as a viewfinder.

stories.title: Microsoft’s Xbox PC app hints at China expansion for Game Pass
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning an expansion of Xbox Game Pass into the Chinese market.
The initiative is referenced internally by the codename "Project Saluki" within the Xbox PC app.
The expansion is expected to cover Game Pass, Rewards, and various subscription tiers.

stories.title: Data centers are coming for rural America
stories.author: Abigail Bassett
bullet_points: Data centers are expanding into rural areas, exemplified by the repurposing of a former paper mill in Jay, Maine.
The story highlights the economic shift in rural America as large-scale tech infrastructure moves into former industrial sites.
The former Androscoggin paper mill, which employed 1,500 people, was purchased in 2023 for data center development.

stories.title: Twin brothers wipe 96 gov't databases minutes after being fired
stories.author: Nate Anderson
bullet_points: The story highlights a security breach where two brothers accessed and potentially compromised 96 government databases.
The incident serves as a case study emphasizing the critical security practice of revoking credentials prior to employee termination.
It points to a significant vulnerability in access control and offboarding procedures within government IT systems.

stories.title: Google's Android-powered laptops are called Googlebooks, and they're coming this year
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is introducing a new line of Android-powered laptops, branded as Googlebooks.
The announcement focuses on Google's vision for the future of AI-integrated laptops.
The devices are slated for release sometime this year.

stories.title: Android is getting a big AI overhaul in 2026
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is planning a major overhaul of the Android operating system.
The core focus of this 2026 update is the integration of Artificial Intelligence.
The announcement signals a significant, long-term strategic direction for Android development.

stories.title: Learning, Fast and Slow: Towards LLMs That Adapt Continually
stories.author: Devvrit Khatri
bullet_points: The paper addresses the trade-off between fine-tuning LLMs (parameter updates) and in-context learning.
Parameter updating risks 'catastrophic forgetting' and limits plasticity due to absorbing task-specific data.
In-context learning offers fast, cheap adaptation but often cannot match the performance of parameter updates.

stories.title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: Proposes a 'Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle' for language model post-training, addressing limitations of standard methods like GRPO.
Argues that current practice of using limited, verifiable data directly on the deployment model is often inefficient.
Suggests allocating sparse sequence-level rewards to models where exploration is productive, while using dense token-level teacher rewards elsewhere.

stories.title: ToolCUA: Towards Optimal GUI-Tool Path Orchestration for Computer Use Agents
stories.author: Jieping Ye
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of suboptimal path planning for Computer Use Agents (CUAs) operating in hybrid GUI/Tool environments.
The core problem is the uncertainty in deciding whether to use low-level GUI actions (click/type) or high-level tool calls (APIs).
The research highlights limitations in current methods due to scarce, high-quality interleaved training data and the cost of real-world data collection.

stories.title: OmniNFT: Modality-wise Omni Diffusion Reinforcement for Joint Audio-Video Generation
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'OmniNFT,' a new framework using Modality-wise Omni Diffusion Reinforcement for joint audio-video generation.
It addresses key challenges in current generation models: ensuring strong per-modality fidelity, cross-modal alignment, and fine-grained synchronization.
The research explores the limitations of applying Reinforcement Learning (RL) to complex, multi-objective, multi-modal generation tasks.

stories.title: Reward Hacking in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: The paper addresses 'reward hacking' specifically within rubric-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
It proposes a framework to reduce reliance on a single training verifier by evaluating policies against a panel of three frontier judges.
The research aims to separate divergence issues into 'verifier failure' and other sources of discrepancy.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a novel, training-free method for long-context inference in large language models.
The technique treats the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator, processing sequence chunks iteratively.
It achieves long-context handling via a one-step recurrence, analogous to a left fold operation.

stories.title: Enabling AI-Native Mobility in 6G: A Real-World Dataset for Handover, Beam Management, and Timing Advance
stories.author: Radha Krishna Ganti
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in current 5G/6G mobility management (handover, beam management) caused by reliance on simulated data.
It proposes the creation of a real-world dataset to accurately model AI/ML techniques for UE mobility in high-speed scenarios.
The work is highly relevant to the transition to 6G, focusing on practical data needs for AI-native network operations.

stories.title: Learning, Fast and Slow: Towards LLMs That Adapt Continually
stories.author: Devvrit Khatri
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of traditional LLM fine-tuning, specifically catastrophic forgetting when updating parameters for new tasks.
It contrasts parameter updating with in-context learning, noting that while in-context learning is fast and cheap, it often cannot match the performance of full parameter updates.
The research aims to develop LLMs capable of continuous adaptation, bridging the gap between rapid in-context learning and deep parameter modification.

stories.title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: Proposes a new 'Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle' for language model post-training.
Challenges the standard practice of using scarce, verifiable training data directly on the deployment model (e.g., via GRPO).
Suggests allocating sparse sequence-level rewards to productive exploration models and dense token-level rewards to teacher models.

stories.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
stories.author: Seong Joon Oh
bullet_points: Introduces MEME, a new evaluation benchmark for LLM-based agents operating in persistent environments.
MEME specifically tests multi-entity and evolving memory capabilities, moving beyond single-entity updates.
Includes novel evaluation tasks like Cascade, Absence, and Deletion to test advanced dependency and state reasoning.

stories.title: Routers Learn the Geometry of Their Experts: Geometric Coupling in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The research addresses challenges in training Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models, specifically routing collapse and load-balancing issues.
The authors reveal a 'geometric coupling' between the token routers and their assigned experts.
This mechanistic understanding aims to improve the specialization and efficiency of large-scale SMoE language models.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a training-free method for long-context inference in large language models.
It utilizes the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator, processing sequence chunks iteratively.
The approach mimics a left fold (foldl) operation, allowing for efficient, step-by-step cache updates.

stories.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance
stories.author: Assaf Toledo
bullet_points: Proposes a novel 'Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement' method using an LLM.
The technique refines user query embeddings in real-time using feedback from a generative LLM on target documents.
Aims to improve the usability of existing embedding models for difficult zero-shot search and classification tasks.

stories.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
stories.author: Seong Joon Oh
bullet_points: Introduces MEME, a new evaluation benchmark for LLM agents operating in persistent environments.
MEME specifically addresses multi-entity and evolving memory, moving beyond single-entity updates.
It introduces novel evaluation tasks like Cascade, Absence, and Deletion to test complex dependency and state reasoning.

stories.title: Routers Learn the Geometry of Their Experts: Geometric Coupling in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The research addresses challenges in training Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models, specifically routing collapse and poor expert specialization.
The core finding reveals a 'geometric coupling' between the routers responsible for token routing and the experts themselves.
This mechanistic study aims to better understand and potentially improve how routing decisions are formed within large-scale SMoE architectures.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a training-free method for enabling long-context inference in large language models.
The technique treats the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator, applying a left-fold operation over sequence chunks.
This one-step recurrence allows the model to process subsequent chunks conditioned on the accumulated cache efficiently.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a novel architecture to improve language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of traditional recurrent architectures (instability, high cost, fixed depth).
The model refines initial output embeddings by solving for a fixed point using an attractor module.

stories.title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models
stories.author: Chao Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of LLMs exhibiting high certainty even when incorrect.
Proposes 'ORCE,' a method for Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in LLMs.
Focuses on generating user-facing uncertainty signals via natural language confidence statements, useful when token logits are unavailable.

stories.title: What Inference-Platform Benchmark Posts Leave Out
stories.author: ingero
bullet_points: The article critiques the limitations of existing inference-platform benchmarks, specifically mentioning DCGM's focus on host-level GPU counters.
It highlights the potential of kernel-side eBPF to provide more granular performance data, such as per-rank and per-tenant signals.
The discussion points toward a need for more detailed and fine-grained monitoring in AI/ML inference infrastructure.
